title: Replace TaskFlow with Move: a retro installable PWA exercise-reminder assistant
description:
# Requirements: Move ŌĆö a sit-less exercise-break reminder (retro PWA)

## Summary
A no-backend Progressive Web App that periodically reminds the user (every configurable X minutes)
to get up and do a short exercise, delivered by a **Clippy-style retro assistant**. Exercises are
chosen from a tag-driven catalog to suit the user's equipment and current context. Users build
**loadouts** (named, shareable filter configs), track progress over time, and can export/import
loadouts as self-contained JSON. Replaces the existing TaskFlow app in this repo, reusing its
scaffolding. All state persists locally and survives reloads ŌĆö export is only for sharing.

## Context & reasoning
Inspired by neuroscientist Erik Scherder's argument that prolonged sitting harms the brain and
that regular movement supports cognition. The goal is to nudge desk-bound people to break up
sitting with brief, context-appropriate exercise, in a fun, deliberately kitschy late-'90s Windows
package. Success = a person opens the site, a sensible default works in seconds, and they get
well-timed, doable reminders that fit their situation and keep them coming back.

## Goal
Ship a usable, installable retro PWA that fires context-aware exercise reminders on a configurable
interval via an assistant character, with a tag-driven catalog, shareable loadouts, and honest
progress tracking ŌĆö all client-side, no backend, auto-persisting.

## Functional requirements
- **Reminders:** configurable interval ("every X minutes"). A reminder fires a browser
  notification while the app is open (foreground or backgrounded); it stops when the app is fully
  closed. Reminders **start paused** on first visit ŌĆö the user opts in via a clear Start control,
  after the notification-permission flow.
- **Assistant persona:** reminders are delivered by a **Clippy-style assistant character** in a
  retro speech bubble (e.g. "It looks like you've been sitting a while. Fancy some squats?"),
  matching the design reference. This persona is a defining part of the UX, not just chrome.
- **Rich notifications:** the OS notification should, where the browser supports it (service-worker
  `showNotification` with actions/image), show the exercise image and inline **Done / Skip /
  Snooze / Shuffle** action buttons. **Graceful fallback:** where actions/images aren't supported,
  show a brief notification that opens the app to the same actions. Never depend on inline actions
  working everywhere.
- **Reminder interaction:** Mark done / Skip / Snooze (remind again shortly) / Shuffle (swap for
  another eligible exercise) ŌĆö available both in-notification (where supported) and in-app.
- **Reminder cue:** play a short **retro Windows-style "ding"** sound when a reminder fires. (No
  vibration.)
- **Exercise catalog:** a built-in curated set, **plus** users can add their own custom exercises.
- **Exercise data model:** each exercise has a name, **form/how-to instructions** (how to do it
  properly), an **image** (ship a generated placeholder per exercise; the user will swap in real
  images/GIFs later ŌĆö so the model references a swappable image asset), a **structured target**
  (reps, e.g. "12 reps", or time, e.g. "hold 30s") shown prominently on the card and in the
  notification, and **tags** (below).
- **Unified tag system:** every exercise carries free-form, **user-extendable tags**. Tags cover
  ALL axes ŌĆö equipment (`pull-up-bar`, `resistance-bands`ŌĆ”), context/location (`office`, `home`ŌĆ”),
  duration/time budget, intensity, body-part/type, and anything users invent. New tags can be
  created on the fly. (User's explicit steer: one scalable tag model, not fixed fields.)
- **Location/context:** a **manual context selector** (e.g. Office / Home / Outdoors) ŌĆö no GPS. The
  active context constrains eligible exercises.
- **Equipment:** the user records which equipment they have; exercises whose required-equipment
  tags aren't satisfied are excluded from the active pool.
- **Loadouts:** a **library of named loadouts** (e.g. "Office", "Home gym", "Travel"); one active
  at a time, user-switchable. A loadout is a set of tag-based rules selecting the eligible pool.
  Filtering must support **include / require-all / exclude** semantics so "requires pull-up bar"
  and "office-safe / never show jumping" both work.
- **Sensible default:** first-time visitors get a ready-to-use default loadout (no equipment,
  office-safe) so they can start immediately without configuring anything.
- **Export / import:** a loadout exports to a **self-contained JSON bundle** ŌĆö the loadout PLUS any
  custom exercises it references, embedded ŌĆö so it works on import even if the recipient lacks
  those. Import merges the bundle in (loadout added to library, custom exercises added to catalog,
  no duplicate clobbering). Export is the ONLY thing that leaves the device, and only on explicit
  user action.
- **Auto-persistence:** ALL state ŌĆö settings, interval, equipment, context, loadouts, custom
  exercises, tags, progress/history ŌĆö saves locally (localStorage) automatically and survives
  reloads. The app must NOT reset to a fresh state on refresh; the user never has to export to keep
  their setup.
- **Progress tracking:** persist **full history + day streaks**, a done count, and an
  **ignored/skipped count**. Plus a **motivational stat** framed **honestly** ŌĆö grounded in real,
  citable movement-break / sedentary-behaviour research (e.g. active minutes reclaimed, sitting
  interrupted). Any brain/cognitive framing is an explicitly playful, clearly labelled estimate ŌĆö
  never an invented number presented as peer-reviewed fact.
- **PWA:** installable, with a web app manifest and a service worker; the static app works offline
  once loaded. (The service worker also backs rich notifications.)

## Technical direction (user's steering)
- **Replace TaskFlow** in this repo, reusing the scaffolding: Vite + React 19 + TS, Tailwind v4
  (CSS-first, already wired), the `src/storage.ts` load/save-with-type-guard-validation pattern,
  the `useBoard`-style "one hook owns state + auto-persist via `useEffect`" pattern, the `types.ts`
  `type` + union-literal modelling, named-function components with local `XProps`, and the Vitest +
  Testing Library setup (`jsdom`, `localStorage.clear()` in `beforeEach`, role/label queries).
  TaskFlow's board/columns/card logic and tests are removed.
- **Visual style follows `ai-design/frontendstyle.jpg`** ŌĆö a deliberately retro late-'90s/early-2000s
  **Windows / MS Office** aesthetic (Clippy-era): beveled 3D buttons, classic gray window chrome,
  yellow speech-bubble balloons, pixel/serif type. Intentionally "not a good frontend" but that
  exact style. (Consult the design reference file directly.)
- **One unified, user-extendable tag system**; loadouts filter with include/require-all/exclude.
- **No backend** ŌĆö static site; reminders are a client timer + service worker; no push server.
- **Manual context**, not GPS.

## Interactions & dependencies
- **Removes** TaskFlow domain code: `src/board.ts`, `src/hooks/useBoard.ts`, `src/components/*`,
  `src/board.test.ts`, and the board-specific parts of `src/App.tsx` / `src/App.test.tsx`.
  **Reuses** the *patterns* in `storage.ts`, the hook, `types.ts`, `index.css` (Tailwind v4 + CSS
  variables), and `src/test/setup.ts`.
- **Reminder engine Ōåö notifications Ōåö service worker Ōåö PWA:** the interval timer, notification
  permission, rich-notification actions, and the SW are interdependent; the SW + manifest are new
  PWA infrastructure layered onto the Vite config. Rich-notification actions specifically require
  the service worker.
- **Assistant persona Ōåö theme Ōåö design reference:** the Clippy-style character and speech-bubble UI
  derive from `ai-design/frontendstyle.jpg`.
- **Tags are the backbone:** catalog, custom exercises, equipment, context, and loadout filtering
  all key off the same tag model ŌĆö designed once, shared.
- **Export/import Ōåö custom exercises Ōåö loadouts:** a shared bundle must embed referenced custom
  exercises; import must merge without duplicating or clobbering.
- **Progress tracking Ōåö reminder actions:** done/skip/snooze/shuffle outcomes feed history,
  streaks, done and ignored counts, and the motivational stat.

## Acceptance criteria
- Fresh visitor: app loads with the default loadout, reminders **paused**, a visible Start control,
  in the retro theme.
- After granting permission and starting, a reminder fires after the interval, delivered by the
  assistant, showing an eligible exercise (respecting active loadout + context + owned equipment)
  with its target, instructions, and (placeholder) image; a retro ding plays.
- Rich notification shows inline Done/Skip/Snooze/Shuffle where supported; where not, a brief
  notification opens the app to the same actions. Each action behaves correctly and updates tracking.
- A user can create a custom exercise (name, instructions, target, image, tags), mint a new tag,
  and see it become eligible when a loadout's filters match.
- Switching the active loadout changes which exercises can appear.
- Exporting a loadout yields JSON containing the loadout + its referenced custom exercises;
  importing on a fresh profile reproduces and runs them.
- Reload does NOT reset state: settings, loadouts, custom exercises, and progress all persist.
- History, streak, done count, and ignored count persist; the motivational stat renders with honest
  framing/citation.
- App is installable as a PWA and loads offline after first load.
- Quality gates pass: `npm run lint` (oxlint), `npm run build` (tsc + vite), new Vitest tests.

## Constraints
- **No backend / static site**; all data local; no accounts, no sync.
- **Privacy:** no geolocation; context is manual; nothing leaves the device except user-initiated
  JSON export.
- **Honesty:** no fabricated statistic presented as science; the motivational stat cites real
  research or is clearly labelled a playful estimate.
- **Notifications** require user permission and only fire while the app runs; rich actions need the
  service worker and degrade gracefully.
- **Stack:** React 19 + TS + Vite + Tailwind v4 (CSS-first); follow existing repo conventions.
- **Design:** match `ai-design/frontendstyle.jpg`; respect `paths.forbidden` and worker/gate rules.

## Out of scope
- Any backend, server, database, or cloud sync.
- True notify-when-fully-closed push (needs a push server) ŌĆö explicitly excluded.
- GPS/automatic location detection.
- Multi-user accounts / auth.
- Native mobile apps (web PWA only).
- Real exercise images/GIFs ŌĆö placeholders ship now; the user adds real assets later.

## Open decisions / assumptions (for the planner to resolve)
- Default reminder interval value (assume ~30 min); snooze duration (assume ~5 min).
- Exercise selection from the eligible pool (assume random with avoid-immediate-repeat).
- Exact motivational-stat metric + cited source/wording (must satisfy the honesty constraint).
- App name/branding (working title "Move"; final name TBD).
- PWA offline scope (assume app-shell caching of the static build).
- Placeholder image format/look (assume a simple generated placeholder per exercise; real GIFs later).
- Exact reps-vs-time target representation (assume one field that expresses either).
